publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, canonical fill-gap, and no code task for Packages and public APIs; every item remains blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Packages and public APIs: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Python: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? A directory with Python files is automatically the same thing as an installable distribution and relative imports work from any launch command.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Compare each option with both the language-specific boundary case and the cited operational model.

feedback.correct: Correct. Distinguish import package from distribution package, define a small public namespace, and launch modules with a valid package context.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. Distinguish import package from distribution package, define a small public namespace, and launch modules with a valid package context.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify source fidelity and ensure the distractor remains plausible without teaching it as fact.

options.text: Distinguish import package from distribution package, define a small public namespace, and launch modules with a valid package context.
A directory with Python files is automatically the same thing as an installable distribution and relative imports work from any launch command.

answer.explanation: Distinguish import package from distribution package, define a small public namespace, and launch modules with a valid package context.
